title: Home
description: ZenithBuild framework homepage and ecosystem overview.

benchmarking.title: Benchmarking is the next validation pass.
body: Zenith is being hardened through real framework and product work first. The next phase is measured comparison under public, repeatable conditions rather than advance performance claims.

That work will compare equivalent pages against established frameworks and publish the methodology alongside the results so bundle size, build behavior, startup cost, and runtime cost are interpreted in context.
The goal is to prove where Zenith is competitive, where tradeoffs exist, and where a different tool may be better suited.

measurements.label: Bundle sizing
measurements.detail: Track shipped client output and page assets for equivalent implementations without folding unrelated application code into the comparison.

measurements.label: Cold build time
measurements.detail: Measure clean builds to show how the compiler and bundling path behave before caches or warmed state reduce the workload.

measurements.label: Dev startup time
measurements.detail: Record the time required to boot a fresh development session so local iteration cost is visible alongside production output.

measurements.label: Rebuild behavior
measurements.detail: Measure change response during active development to show how quickly edits propagate and where rebuild overhead appears.

measurements.label: Hydration and runtime cost
measurements.detail: Inspect the browser-side work required to make equivalent pages interactive, including hydration scope and runtime execution surface.

measurements.label: Equivalent page comparisons
measurements.detail: Use comparable page shapes and feature sets so the benchmark measures framework behavior rather than mismatched workloads.

measurements.label: Established framework comparisons
measurements.detail: Compare Zenith against widely used frameworks with methodology and environment details published beside the results.

methodology.label: Methodology Commitment
methodology.title: Results will be published with context, not headlines.
methodology.text: Each benchmark will document what was measured, what the metric represents, how the setup was reproduced, and where the comparison should be interpreted carefully. Zenith is being positioned as a tool for specific needs, not as a universal winner across every workload.
